what_it_does: Records a channel attachment metadata entry that links attachment att-522698ce to board item item-b1cc22b7 in the dragnet-development channel.
observations: YAML frontmatter declares id, channel, attachment_type, target, and added timestamp.
target uses a board:// URI scheme pointing to item-b1cc22b7.
File body is empty after the frontmatter.
standouts: The target is a board://item-b1cc22b7 URI, indicating cross-entity linkage rather than a file path.
It is an empty-body metadata attachment record.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with leading and trailing --- delimiters.
Markdown file used as a structured metadata carrier.
IDs use an att- prefix with a hex slug.
References to external entities use a custom board:// URI scheme.
concerns: No content beyond metadata; purpose depends entirely on external board item resolution.
No author or modification history is captured.
notes: This is a lightweight metadata stub that attaches a board item reference to the dragnet-development channel.
questions: What content or artifact does board://item-b1cc22b7 represent?
How is the attachment_type board_items consumed by the system?
Is the added timestamp authoritative or derived from file creation?
purpose_vs_actual: The file appears to serve as a linkage record between a channel and a board item, which matches its metadata-only content; stated purpose is implicit but consistent.
intention_signals: It seems intended to persist a durable, timestamped association between a channel discussion context and a specific board item for later retrieval or rendering.
novelty: The use of a board:// URI as a target in attachment metadata is a distinct referencing pattern.
contribution: Provides a stable, timestamped channel-to-board-item association that a fused system can traverse when reconstructing context or attachment lineage.
summary_for_embedding: A channel attachment metadata file in the dragnet-development channel linking attachment att-522698ce to board item item-b1cc22b7 via a board:// URI with an added timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: To record when and how a board item was attached to a channel conversation as a retrievable reference.
